title: serrafalcus racemosus subsp. commutatus
intro: Serrafalcus racemosus subsp. commutatus, also known as the Clustered Saw-wort, is a type of flowering plant in the genus Serrafalcus, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Serrafalcus racemosus subsp. commutatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Serrafalcus racemosus subsp. commutatus can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions.
